picks.title: Homelanding
picks.blurb: A free summer festival on the waterfront Marineterrein — food, music, a swim spot and design across the old naval yard.

picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: POISED w/ Force Reaction, Oxygeno, OZUM, Joey Marcel, Raeon
picks.blurb: Oxygeno, Force Reaction, Kaiser (K S R), Kwartz and more at TILLATEC.

picks.title: Madam by Night invites: VELVET
picks.blurb: HOTBOX COLLECTIVE, Lidia Lo, Sanne Dammers at Madam.

picks.title: Carel Visser in de Rijksmuseumtuinen
picks.blurb: Sculptural exhibition featuring renowned Dutch artist Carel Visser's works displayed in the museum's gardens.

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition 2026
picks.blurb: Global photojournalism exhibition showcasing the year's most impactful news photography and visual storytelling.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Gohan
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Studio/K, LAB111, Rialto, Ketelhuis, Filmha
picks.blurb: Touching story of a stray dog touching lives across a decade. Heartwarming friendship and human-animal bond.

picks.title: Monne
picks.blurb: Mediterranean restaurant by the water with boat dock; spacious terrace perfect for families.
picks.why: Waterfront dining escape from the city

picks.title: Nara Nara
picks.blurb: New Egyptian restaurant by chef Said; sharing plates with Cairo flavours and Mediterranean influences.

picks.title: The Invite
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Kriterion, Studio-K, LAB111, Rialto, FC Hye
picks.blurb: Dinner party spirals into chaos revealing hidden desires and secrets. Darkly funny, awkward, surprisingly relatable.

picks.title: Bostheater
picks.blurb: Outdoor theater in the forest offering diverse summer performances including family shows and live music.

picks.title: The Odyssey
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Kriterion, Studio-K, LAB111, Rialto, FC Hye
picks.blurb: Odysseus battles mythical creatures and sirens on perilous journey home after Troy. Epic IMAX spectacle.
picks.why: Homer's classic comes alive on massive screen

picks.title: Moana/Vaiana (Live Action)
picks.blurb: Animated classic becomes live-action epic. Vaiana sails beyond reef with Maui in stunning adventure.
picks.why: Live-action reimagining of beloved family film

picks.title: ENTR VR Museum
picks.blurb: Step into 17th-century Amsterdam via VR headset—walk streets, meet residents, and shape the story with your choices.
picks.why: Time travel through Amsterdam's Golden Age

picks.title: Finding Emily
picks.blurb: Lovesick musician teams with psych student searching for mystery girl. Campus chaos with unexpected chemistry.

picks.title: The Jollof Club
picks.blurb: West African restaurant by chef Nicolas Aquaa with modern dishes from Ghana, Nigeria, Senegal.
picks.why: Modern West African cuisine with storytelling

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch films like Chef or Ratatouille while eating exactly what's on screen. Cinematic fine dining experience.

picks.title: Kay Slice QUANZA
picks.blurb: Free outdoor concert in Vondelpark. Bring a picnic blanket and enjoy music under the summer sky.

picks.title: Man in the Mirror
picks.blurb: Michael Jackson tribute with live band, dancers, and iconic choreography bringing the King of Pop back to life.

picks.title: La Petite Dernière
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Studio-K, FC Hyena, Ketelhuis, The Movies,
picks.blurb: French-Algerian teen discovers freedom and first love in Paris while navigating family and faith.
picks.why: Coming-of-age drama about identity and love

picks.title: Hortus Summer Tours
picks.blurb: Guided botanical tour past greenhouses & hidden gems, terrace drinks & live music after.

picks.title: De Hortus Zomeravonden
picks.blurb: Botanical garden open until 20:00 with live music, tours, and terrace with drinks.
picks.why: Garden evening with live music and golden hour

picks.title: Homelanding Zomerfestival
picks.blurb: Five-day free festival with live music, food, workshops, kids' activities, and performances.
picks.why: Free festival with food and entertainment

picks.title: RadioRadioOatlyOatly Pop-up
picks.blurb: Relaxed outdoor summer hangout with DJs, live radio broadcasts, signature drinks.
picks.why: Summer pop-up with music and good vibes

picks.title: Openluchttheater Vondelpark
picks.blurb: Free outdoor theater season featuring diverse performances: music, dance, cabaret. Perfect for summer picnics.

picks.title: Garlic Festival at Restaurant VRR
picks.blurb: Garlic-themed market with live music and food stalls; three-course dinner at 16:30.
picks.why: Garlic celebration with market and dinner

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Maritime museum exhibition exploring Japan's visual narratives and cultural connections through historical archives.

picks.title: Game On
picks.blurb: Interactive exhibition exploring how video games shape play, learning, connection, and self-expression for all ages.

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore centuries of Dutch-Japanese cultural exchange through ceremonial swords, lacquered boxes, and decorative screens.

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Personal exhibition exploring identity and self-expression through photographs, self-portraits, and childhood objects.

picks.title: Stoornis of my life
picks.blurb: Musical with Alex Klaasen exploring life, autism, and family change through humor, heart, and live music.

picks.title: Rembrandt het leven
picks.blurb: Exhibition exploring Rembrandt's life and artistic legacy within his historic Amsterdam house-studio.

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End musical based on true story of Henry Fraser, exploring life-changing moments with emotion and hope.

picks.title: Spinoza
picks.blurb:
Amsterdam, 17th century. 
 A city that sees itself as a safe haven. 
 A Portuguese-Jewish refugee community. 
 Three children. One family. 
 About Spinoza
 Bent
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: TREK Foodfestival
picks.blurb: Open-air food festival with foodtrucks, live music, theatre, and kermis attractions.
picks.why: Free food festival with live entertainment

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Land Art Weekend
picks.blurb: World's largest concentration of land art with guided tours, exhibition on water, cello concert, photography workshop, poetry performance.
picks.why: Unique outdoor art experience, guided tours, multiple activi

picks.title: ARTIS Summer Nights
picks.blurb: Summer Saturdays at ARTIS: live music in the bandstand, animals at dusk, flowers and greenery.
picks.why: Free with zoo entry, Saturdays through August

picks.title: Vondelpark Open Air Theatre
picks.blurb: Free open-air performances including comedy, dance, music, cabaret. Part of WorldPride programming. No ticket required.
picks.why: Free performances, 50+ year tradition, WorldPride events

picks.title: Open Podium Noord
picks.blurb:
Accessible stage
 Open Podium Noord is free to attend. This makes the event accessible to everyone. The performances vary each week, depending on the artists wh

picks.title: Une femme est une femme
picks.blurb:
Movie story
 Discover the story of Angela, a dancer in Paris who wants to have children. However, her boyfriend Émile is hesitant to start a family. This leads

picks.title: StandOut's Arabic Comedy Showcase Show
picks.blurb:
Let the good vibes roll
 Expect a high-energy atmosphere with a rotating cast of rising stars and seasoned pros, all sharing the stage to deliver their best mat

picks.title: Filippo & Aruth - Black & White Cuphead
picks.blurb:
Scala food bar theater
 Scala offers you a unique evening out: combine dining with 20-minute performances. The shows span various genres, from cabaret to music

picks.title: Julidans
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's international dance festival showcasing cutting-edge choreography and movement across multiple venues.
picks.why: Major festival running through the weekend

picks.title: Festival TREK Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Netherlands' largest traveling food festival with food trucks, live music, theater and small-scale performers. Transforms park into open-air restaurant.
picks.why: Free entry, food, music, theater all in one

picks.title: Vunzige Deuntjes Festival
picks.blurb: Urban culture festival with 20,000 visitors, 6+ stages celebrating dancehall, hip-hop, house, disco. Food, culture market, dance battles.
picks.why: 20,000 visitors, massive main stage, DJs and live acts

picks.title: Cheeky Monday
picks.blurb:
Always a cool line-up!
 The Amsterdam crew runs one of the longest running club nights in Europe here, and is known worldwide for its great atmosphere, thanks i

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb:
Art, craft and design
 Museum Market takes place every month, every 3rd Sunday of the month (in October the 4th Sunday of the month). Between the trees near the

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: Art Amsterdam Spui
picks.blurb:
Discover art that truly touches you
 At Art Amsterdam Spui, artists present and sell their work themselves — straight from the studio and in direct contact with

picks.title: BOPS queer. pop. party
picks.blurb:
BOPS queer. pop. party
 Lineup: Resident DJs TMORGZ and Absoluut + various guest DJs
 BOPS is a space for the queer community and allies to dance the night away

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Ten Katemarkt
picks.blurb: A proper everyday neighbourhood market off the tourist track — heaped produce, street food and Oud-West locals doing the shop.

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Lindengrachtmarkt
picks.blurb: The Jordaan’s beloved Saturday market — farm cheese, flowers and Amsterdam’s best market-stall fish, all down a leafy street.
